mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and reduce present value of future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of stable unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous peaks in retail sales and housing starts indicate maximum consumer leverage and demand exhaustion, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series are in the top quartile, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.01) when INDPRO growth exceeds 2% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained labor market expansion and rising aggregate income, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when PAYEMS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Annualized real GDP growth falling below 2.0 percent signals weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of observations when GDP growth drops below the threshold, or the predictive relationship breaks down during financial crisis periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and rising unemployment indicates inefficient capacity utilization and structural labor market weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression, or forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation readings above 200 on the CPI index trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, or Rank IC drops below 0.03 during periods of stable inflation expectations.
